message:
test(config): pin deferred-recovery cleanup for the missing-db case

Companion test to commit 97a085c which wired the missing-database
deferred-recovery path into the pending-recovery-cleanup machinery
so an import failure against a freshly-created DB (created only
because the deferred-recovery strategy wanted a handle) removes the
fresh DB file on restore instead of leaving it behind.

Test body:
  1. Create .beads/ and issues.jsonl only (no .beads/beads.db).
  2. Call `open_storage_with_cli_deferred_jsonl_recovery`.
  3. Assert `pending_recovery_dir()` is Some — cleanup is tracked.
  4. Assert beads.db now exists — deferred-recovery created it.
  5. Call `restore_pending_recovery_backup()`.
  6. Assert beads.db is gone again — matches pre-open state.
  7. Assert the recovery_dir is empty / absent.

Pins the exact invariant the production fix introduces: a caller
opening via the deferred-recovery strategy can always roll back
to the pre-open filesystem state by calling
`restore_pending_recovery_backup`, regardless of whether the
original DB existed or was missing.

Cargo.lock intentionally not committed — local path-override
artifact.
